assertions.text: I was traveling at about 90 km/h.
I was traveling at highway speed.
I struck the back of a parked vehicle with my front bumper (I hit the parked vehicle from behind).
I hit the brakes only at the last moment before impact.
There was a single impact with the parked vehicle.
My vehicle's front bumper struck the parked vehicle (implying a frontal-to-rear collision likely more than a minor tap).
I was traveling at speed and then hit the brakes only immediately before making contact with the parked vehicle (braked just prior to impact).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows the vehicle speed was around [34.0 km/h](T2) at t=-5.0 s and [32.0 km/h](T2) at t=0 s, far below [90 km/h](T1).'

The recorded speeds are in the mid-30s km/h: [max 36.0 km/h](T2) and [last 32.0 km/h](T2), which is well below typical highway speeds and the vehicle's [speed_limit_kmh 48.0](T1) noted for this vehicle.

The sensors record a longitudinal delta-V of [−18.0 km/h](T2) during impact, indicating a rearward change in vehicle velocity along a device axis, but device-frame axis alignment to vehicle front/back is undocumented so direction of impact (struck the back of another vehicle with front bumper) is [−18.0 km/h](T2) evidence that cannot by itself determine front-versus-rear contact.

Brake status transitions show the brake was off until a transition to on at [t = −0.5 s](T2) and the vehicle was moving at [32.0 km/h](T2) at impact, consistent with braking only immediately before impact.
EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded 1](T1) and the delta-V time history shows a single crash pulse with final cumulative longitudinal delta-V [−18.0 km/h](T2), consistent with a single impact.

There is a cumulative longitudinal delta-V of [−18.0 km/h](T2) indicating a moderate impact, but telemetry alone cannot confirm which vehicle structure (front bumper) made contact or whether it was more than a minor tap; structure/contact details are unverifiable from sensors.

Speed samples show the vehicle was traveling at [34.0 km/h](T2) to [32.0 km/h](T2) in the seconds before impact and the brake changed from off to on at [t = −0.5 s](T2), indicating the braking occurred just prior to contact.

result.rationale: Telemetry contradicts the claim of traveling at about [90 km/h](T1) and highway speeds; it supports that braking occurred immediately before a single impact with a longitudinal delta-V of [−18.0 km/h](T2). Axis alignment and contact location (front bumper to parked vehicle rear) are unverifiable from device-frame sensors.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
